notes: The Canaanites were a superstitious people who believed in and practiced divination and black magic. An enchanter inspects the entrails of dead animals, watches the flight of birds, or used other means to predict the future. A charmer employs spells and incantations in predicting future events. Consulters with familiar spirits try to contact the spirit of a departed person to learn things not known to human beings. A wizard is a male witch. A necromancer, like one who consults with familiar spirits, seeks the secrets of the spirit world by inquiring of the dead. All of these activities were forbidden to ancient Israel. They were admonished to heed the words of their living prophet. (CES Old Testament Student Manual: Genesis-2 Samuel, 226)
perfect (tamiym) complete, whole, entire, sound, healthful

Many in attendance at the miraculous feeding identified Jesus as "that prophet that should come into the world" (John 6:14). This relates to Deuteronomy 18:15-18, wherein Moses prophesies of the future prophet that God will raise up like unto him. The "great company" then sought to take Jesus "by force, to make him a king" (John 6:15). (Thomas R. Valletta, "The True Bread of Life," Ensign, Mar. 1999, 10)

notes: "When is a prophet a prophet? Whenever the speaks under the inspiration and influence of the Holy Ghost. … "When prophets write and speak on the principles of the gospel, they should have the guidance of the Spirit. If they do, then all that they say will be in harmony with the revealed word. If they are in harmony then we know that they have not spoken presumptuously. Should a man speak or write, and what he
says is in conflict with the standards which are accepted, with the revelations the Lord has given, then we may reject what he has said, no matter who he is." (Smith, Doctrines of Salvation, 1:187.)
